requirements.statement: Record that the repo implementation is complete enough to pass `nightly-full` locally.
Record that the shared sandbox runtime, endpoint, bootstrap resources, artifact upload, and stack deployment have succeeded in AWS account `530267068969`.

Record that the remaining live blocker is enforced SCP guard application because the available local credentials do not have Organizations policy-management permissions for management account `023016403424`.
Record the exact follow-on tasks needed to complete deployment close-out once management-account access is available.
Keep the final completion path compatible with redeploying the same solution into a fresh environment by configuration and IaC rather than manual rebuild.
Make management-account profile or `--assume-role-arn` wiring an explicit completion item so deploy plus live guard enforcement can be run as one deterministic workflow.

evidence_inventory.summary: One shared runtime executes both PP and SDLC workflows with common audit and delegation plumbing.
AgentCore entrypoint exposes `/ping` and `/invocations` for the shared runtime.
Repository contains the strict `nightly-full` quality-gate lane used as the close-out baseline.
Bootstrap script resolves or creates runtime/endpoints, deploys the shared stack, and invokes the AWS guard script.
Guard script already supports `--assume-role-arn`, validates stack outputs and endpoint state, and enforces SCPs when Organizations access exists.
IaC bootstrap template provisions the artifact bucket and AgentCore runtime execution role needed for fresh-environment deployment.
Automated tests cover runtime reuse/creation, IaC bootstrap provisioning, and endpoint convergence behavior.

evidence_inventory.source: Current workspace and prior verified deploy run in this session
evidence_inventory.summary: Shared sandbox runtime, endpoint, and `FlutterAgentCorePocStack` are already deployed in account `530267068969`; guard output shows stack/runtime checks pass while SCP enforcement remains blocked.
Available local profiles for account `530267068969` fail SCP lookup with `AccessDeniedException`, confirming missing management-account permissions.
Guard tests already cover clean handling of Organizations access-denied failures.
README already documents bootstrap of the shared sandbox, but still contains stale text earlier that the repo is not currently a working runtime implementation.

sources.trust_rationale: Primary source for current implementation, tests, bootstrap scripts, and documentation.
Primary live source for current deploy state and Organizations permission boundaries observed from available credentials.

notes: The management-account dependency is operational, not architectural; it should not trigger redesign of the shared-platform topology.
A useful close-out improvement is to wire management-account access into the bootstrap command so live SCP enforcement becomes part of the deterministic deploy contract.
README deployment guidance now lags the implemented runtime/bootstrap state and should be aligned during completion close-out.
